ribbon_development_rule: 5 or more houses on one side of any 250 m of road frontage treated as problematic ribbon development
residential_density_dph: Targets by area, e.g. City Centre 100; Outer Suburbs 35–60 dph
separation_distance_m: 22 m between rear elevations (traditional minimum); lesser often appropriate in urban context
private_open_space: At least 48 sq.m (houses); smaller acceptable with quality justification
building_height: City Centre target 4–6 storeys; South Docks up to 10 storeys for exceptional tall buildings
trigger_AA: All applications screened in respect of European Sites (Natura 2000)
trigger_EIA: All applications screened; may be required below thresholds where significant effects likely
trigger_FRA: Referenced (e.g. Traveller accommodation excludes Flood Zones A/B); detailed policy elsewhere in plan
trigger_AHIA: Built-heritage statements may be required (protected structures / ACAs)
trigger_EcIA: Ecological reports may be required for environmentally sensitive areas
trigger_VIA: For developments affecting protected views or breaking the city skyline
trigger_TTA: Required where excessive impact on road/transport network likely; mandatory for access to national road network
notes: Cork City predominantly urban; rural one-off minimum site area not explicitly quantified. No numeric parking/plot ratio/site coverage/EV/bin figures in captured Chapter 11. Daylight/sunlight for 50+ units; RIA for retail over 1,000 sq.m net.
